description_long: This sports center is a large, rectangular building with a prominent terracotta-colored hip roof that encloses a central open courtyard or atrium. The roof features multiple ridges and gables, with several dormer-like structures and a stepped section on one side, suggesting possible skylights or ventilation. The exterior walls are light-colored, likely concrete or stucco, and are lined with evenly spaced windows along the longer facades, providing ample natural light to the interior. The building includes a distinct entrance block on one corner, which projects outward and may house a lobby or reception area. The overall design is symmetrical and functional, with a focus on maximizing interior space and accessibility. The structure is typical of modern urban sports centers, blending utilitarian design with subtle architectural detailing.
description: A large, rectangular sports center with a terracotta hip roof, central courtyard, and light-colored walls lined with windows. Features a prominent entrance block and symmetrical, functional design.
description_view_0: The main facade is visible with the projecting entrance block on the right and a clear view of the hip roof and central courtyard.
description_view_1: This view shows the shorter side of the building, highlighting the symmetrical window arrangement and the entrance block on the left.
description_view_2: The rear facade is visible, showing the stepped roof section and the continuation of the window-lined walls.
description_view_3: The opposite short side is shown, with the entrance block now on the right and the roof's gabled features clearly visible.
attribute_entrance_type: Single main entrance block with likely double doors, projecting from the main facade; no other entrance types visible.
attribute_roof_structure: Hip roof with terracotta tiles, multiple ridges and gables, stepped section on one side, and possible dormer or skylight features.
attribute_window_arrangement: Rows of evenly spaced rectangular windows along the longer facades, symmetrically aligned; no floor-to-ceiling or grouped windows visible.
attribute_architectural_style: Contemporary utilitarian with modernist influences, emphasizing function and symmetry.
